name: Aelbon
description: Aelbon is a lush territory nestled in southern Inverland, known for its rich natural resources, fertile fields, and thriving wildlife. The territory is home to the enigmatic Populi Verde, a nature-worshipping group with a troubled history. Aelbon is known as home to two grand cities, Dha Chaomhnoir on the coast and Blackfield in the northeastern hills.

name: Aikshaw Descent
description: Stretching along the southern face of the Eustera Ridge Mountains, Aikshaw Descent offers a variety of landscapes, ranging from towering peaks to forested valleys. The territory is home to the fishing city of Abereth on the shores of Loch Cerule and Bonfaduhr, stronghold of the Oreguard dwarves.

name: Aravae
description: Tucked along the southeastern edge of Orbansia, Aravae is a rugged land with dense forests that are green through much of the year. Its steep cliffs rise above the crashing waves of the Embueril Ocean, formed in part by the distant presence of the smoldering Mount Feutagne. Most of the settlements in Aravae are scattered and often secluded, separated by vast woods and accessible only by winding paths.

name: Arbescar
description: Arbescar is a land of striking contrasts, where the scorching winds of the Blustery Waste give way to lush, emerald forests and sparkling river valleys. Once isolated from the mainland, it was joined to Myrdae by the Awakening's upheaval, now connected through the southernmost stretch of the Dragonspine Mountains. The territory bears the lasting mark of the archmage Tsuul'physs Bhan, whose legacy still echoes in the land's shifting magic, ancient ruins, and whispered lore.

name: Baltwood
description: Baltwood is a rugged territory in eastern Orbansia, nestled between the northern reaches of the Glimmering Sea and the southeastern edge of the Distancion Mountains. The territory is dominated by the vast and ancient Baltwood Forest and shadowed by the mildly active Mount Emberstran, a volcanic peak. The territory's most prominent settlements include Emberstran, and the dwarven city Dunduar.

name: Barrford
description: Far to the east of Inverland, where the Tideless Span gives way to the open High Sea, lies Barrford — a quiet territory defined by rugged foothills and wide coastal plains. The northeastern reaches of the Eustera Ridge Mountains fall off into rolling hills, while the coastal lands provide rich fishing waters and windswept grasslands. Barrford is sparsely settled, with the modest city of Stinson serving as its capital and primary hub of trade, governance, and seafaring life.

name: Blisterfel
description: Blisterfel is a cold territory where the land is frozen three harmons out of the year. The land is in the northernmost reaches of Inverland, where icy winds sweep across jagged ridgelines and snow-covered valleys. Most of the hardy people of this frigid land live in Avelvein, Blisterfel's largest city. Perpetually shrouded in frost, the land is home to ancient ruins of long-lost civilizations buried beneath glacial sheets.

name: Borealian Sway
description: The Borealian Sway is a territory rich with ancient groves, cascading rivers, and terraced foothills. It is most known for being home to the Elle'berian, the first elven settlers and the founders of the territory's capital city, Qal'dynn. Settlements in the territory are guarded and watched by the elven watch known as the Realmwardens. Though the land is welcoming to outsiders, its rulers ensure that foreign influences do not disrupt the region's balance.

name: Camburne
description: Camburne spans the western edge of Inverland, where lush plains, vibrant wetlands, and dense forests meet the crashing waves of the Delisnor Ocean. The territory is known for its strikingly colorful flora and the native gertot — wooly, horned ungulates whose prized fleece drives Camburne's thriving wool trade.

name: Caristone
description: Caristone is a largely untamed land between the Distancion Mountains and Dragonspine Mountains, with rolling hills, rich forests, and glimmering rivers. One of the area's most well known features is the Starlight Cradle, a wide valley studded with luminous crystal outcroppings believed to be from the Broken Moon and home to the town of Boldshire. Once the site of the original eastern gate to the dwarven stronghold of Dunduar, the Awakening sealed the mountain passage, shifting all access to the other side of the summit.

name: Corse of Veinspear
description: A land defined by its numerous islands on the northwest coast of Jeshle, the Corse of Veinspear can be a dangerous place. Pirates and merchants alike seek "Drift Keys," rare magical compasses attuned to a shifting seabed where arcane tides and magical currents shift the islands' positions. Navigation often demands tide-lore, ancient star charts, or specialized relics.

name: Crimson March
description: The Crimson March rose from the filled chasm of the Crungess during the Awakening, now blanketed by towering evergreens known as Crimson Pines — named for their blood-red needles said to grow from soil soaked by centuries of warfare. Once a battleground for countless conflicts, the land pulses with memory, its woods haunted by the restless spirits of warriors who died without honor or purpose. Travelers speak of ghostly figures stalking the mist and whispers that echo like battle cries deep among the trees.

name: Dalasor Coast
description: The Dalasor Coast forms the southernmost edge of Orbansia, set between the warm waters of the Dalasor Strait and the border of Aravae. Its landscape shifts from dense woodlands and sprawling wetlands to arid coastal hills.

name: Dunmoore
description: In southeastern Inverland, Dunmoore is full of rolling grasslands and low hills that stretch to the Tideless Span and the Sea of Sondera. Its most striking feature, the Bluffs of Dunmoore, have steep plateaus surrounded by a series of floating isles that drift above the land — separated from the land during the Awakening and becoming home to numerous Aarakocra settlements.

name: Esgaar
description: Esgaar is a frigid territory in southern Nalscour, hemmed in by mountains to the north and the waters of the Broken Deep to the south. Jagged ice fields and rugged fjords define the land, but its most iconic feature is the Nalscour Runs — deep, ancient channels carved through rock and glacier, said to mark the paths of primordial beasts that once shaped the land.

name: Flarebane Isles
description: Scattered across the heart of the Broken Deep, the Flarebane Isles are jagged remnants of divine ruin — born when the god Brault was cast from the heavens during the Fracturing. One of the larger isles is said to have the Flarebane Sigil, a once-radiant celestial seal now cracked and dark, leaking demonic energy that twists the sea and land of the isles whenever prayers are offered to the new gods. The isles are home to many pirate strongholds, monstrous sea beasts, and cursed ruins.

name: Frystarian Strand
description: The Frystarian Strand is the northernmost reach of the Bathaen Empire, where cliffs and forests meet the waves of the Broken Deep and High Sea. Centuries ago, the mages of the Tegenwald ignited the Shroudburn — vast magical blazes that scorched away the dense wilderness and drove out the shadowy creatures that lurked within. The land still bears the scars and blessings of that fire, with warm mist rising from the land and ancient stones said to whisper remnants of forgotten spells.

name: Gaelscape
description: Gaelscape is a wild and vibrant territory east of the Distancion Mountains, where the Awakening drove back the Mahruud and a deep connection to the Feywild was reborn. Dense forests and radiant meadows now teem with fey creatures. Scattered throughout the land are the ancient Oathcourts — mystic sites where ancestral bargains with the fey were once forged, and where old promises now stir back to life.

name: Glenleah
description: Glenleah is a fertile and mist-veiled heartland in central Inverland, stretching from rolling green hills to the shores of the great Loch Cerule. The city of Gibuldon, Inverland's largest, sits at the lake's edge — its stone halls and ivy-wrapped towers steeped in tradition, music, and old magic. Atop sacred Draebaen plateau in the Glenleah Woods burns the Fire of Ahln'Drae, an eternal flame that is rekindled each Salvus through storm-born lightning, said to guard Glenleah's soul.

name: Graelaug
description: Graelaug is a cold, low-lying coastal territory on the eastern edge of the Broken Sea, blanketed in icy forests. Its people are hardy seafarers and warriors, shaped by ancient traditions. Scattered across the land of Graelaug are the Frostmarks — mystic stones of unknown age that are said to glow faintly only for those in favor of the gods.

name: Halesworth
description: Halesworth is the heart of the Bathaen Empire and the most populous territory in Myrdae, centered around the grand city of Basctdelm — a hub of commerce, politics, and imperial power. From the lowlands, to the coast, to the high passes of the Distancion Mountains, the land is known for its structured rule and a deep-rooted legacy of the high perched Uktali Monastery. Halesworth and Wheldrake share Basctdelm as a capital.

name: Hillock Torn
description: Hillock Torn is a quiet, rugged territory shaped by two wide valleys nestled among the foothill ridgelines that stretch from the Distancion Mountains. It's known for its Standing Folds — towering stone formations shaped by ancient tectonic force, whose curved faces carry echoes and songs with distinct clarity. The Dalerun and Northern Stoneflow Rivers cut through the center of the large valleys.

name: Khasvadia
description: Khasvadia is a shadowed, mist-laden territory on the eastern edge of Myrdae where crumbling manors cling to hillsides and ancient woods conceal secrets. The land is ruled in fear by Countess Riah Nas Verdidra, a figure whose presence haunts the people more than any law or throne. It is said that those who die in terror within Khasvadia become Mistbound — silent, lingering spirits drawn to her darkness, unable to pass on until the Countess herself is unmade.

name: Kholdur
description: Kholdur, located in northern Orbansia, is full of foothills and rivers that flow from the Distancion Mountains to meet the Sea of Sondera. The land is known for Thel Kholdur, a grand dwarven city carved deep into the mountains with a long underground pass that leads to the Bathaen Empire beneath.

name: Lyewell Stretch
description: Lyewell Stretch marks the westernmost edge of Inverland, where jagged landmasses known as the Tidewoven Rise extend into the Delisnor Ocean like coral-formed fingers, lifted during the Awakening. Beneath these high bluffs, bone-colored reefs shaped from wreckage and sailor's remains form eerie underwater forests. The port city of Porpen stands as Myrdae's western gateway, where traders, mystics, and wanderers arrive from distant shores.

name: Mahruud
description: The Mahruud is a scorched and craggy territory in northeastern Myrdae, where blighted winds howl through ravines and broken plateaus. Long shaped by dark magic and violent coups, its capital Thrargael stands as its center of ruthless ambition. The desolate Mahruud is known for its harsh conditions and dangersous inhabitants.

name: Matahal Valley
description: Matahal Valley lies at the heart of Orbansia, cradled between two mountain ranges and known for the wetlands that dominate its southeastern expanse. Mist-laden lowlands give way to fertile highland ridges. Though rich in resources, the valley remains elusive — its hidden paths protecting it from outsiders.

name: Mulshear
description: Resting along the eastern coast of Orbansia, where the Saltbay Sea meets the Embueril Ocean, is the territory of Mulshear. Its northern reaches offer rolling grasslands, wooded hills, and scattered settlements. But to the south, the land sinks into dense swamps and murky wetlands, perpetually veiled in heavy fog. Dark tales warn travelers to avoid these southern marshes, for it is said that the undead roam under the will of a sinister figure known only as the Soul Reaper.

name: Nandahere
description: Nandahere is the southernmost territory in Myrdae, a land of dense, vibrant tropical forests full of rare flora. The territory is situated in Jehsle just south of Mount Wick and separated from Orbansia by the shimmering waters of the Dalasor Strait.

name: Okodia
description: Okodia sits along the northwestern edge of Orbansia, where the Tideless Span widens into the Sea of Sondera. Towering in the north are the Wayward Crest of the Distancion Mountains, while the sweeping Bay of Visions dominates the southern coastline. Rolling hills, forests, open plains, and rocky shores make up the rest of the Okodian landscape. Skrettel, the weefolk city, is the capital perched high on a plateau beneath the looming Grand Peaks.

name: Qae'thesvorin
description: Qae'thesvorin is the largest territory in Jehsle, stretching along the Sea of Sondera and the Sea of Marilia. Towering mesas, volcanic ridges, and wind-carved highlands dominate the landscape. Revered and feared across Myrdae, Qae'thesvorin is home to the revered and feared draconian ancestries.

name: Range of Otesurr
description: The Range of Otesurr is a rugged northeastern territory of Myrdae, defined by the towering Otesurr Mountains that run from north to south, cleaving the land between the churning Broken Sea and the vast Embueril Ocean. The mountains are steep and jagged with teeth and only a handful of ancient passes link the two sides of the territory.

name: Roaring Hollow
description: The Roaring Hollow is a snowbound coastal territory northeast of Eustera Ridge and the Deadwing Mountains, and reaching out to the High Sea. In the far north lies the Manus Peninsula, a rugged expanse of sea cliffs and frost-slick caves where salt-laced crystals hum with ancient resonance — believed to hold echoes of the land's forgotten past.

name: Rostiena Foothills
description: The Rostiena Foothills is a territory in Orbansia, defined by its hills, forests and the mighty Stoneflow River that carves a path between the Glimmering Sea and the Sea of Sondera. At the river's mouth sits Stoneshore, the bustling capital whose harbors open into Greywater Bay — making it a vital hub of trade and travel.

name: Sarbaugh
description: A frigid, icebound island in the High Sea, home to the Ghecbaurg Clan (Whitebear in common), a brutal and war-hardened orc tribe that endures the relentless cold within craggy settlements and frozen caves.

name: Serriveil
description: The serene territory of Serriveil, in northern Orbansia, is known for its lush, expansive forest called Shademeadow that sits in the shadow of the Meadowgarde ridge line. The land is protected by an ancient covenant between druids and nature itself, upheld by the Elderoot — sentient trees that watch in silence and defend the balance of the forest.

name: Severed Bend
description: Tucked between the Glimmering Sea and Siltbay, the Severed Bend is a territory in northeastern Orbansia where the Severed Crease range descends rolling hills and rocky coastal flats. Lacking grand cities, the Severed Bend carries a quiet resilience — its people preferring smaller towns and villages.

name: Sutherford
description: Sutherland is a southern Inverland territory that stretches from the Delisnor Ocean to the inland waters of Lake Celemere. The southern coast thrives on fishing and maritime trade, while the northern Gilburough Timbers offer dense, hardy woodland known for its lumber and game. Between the Midram Basin and the forested uplands, Sutherford is a land balanced between industry and wilderness.

name: Tegenwald
description: Recently re-established as a recognized territory, Tegenwald is a land where nature and magic seem deeply intertwined. Thick forests, lakes, and rugged coastlines stretch out across the land, but none dominate the skyline like the towering Mount Everbloom, a vast dormant volcano. Long whispered to be one of the strongest nexuses of the Weave in all of Myrdae, many believe the land itself hums with ancient arcane energy.

name: Theamis
description: Theamis is a varied and resource-rich territory bounded by the Glimmering Sea to the north and the Kaltera Mountains to the west. The cities of Glimmerstone and Adamont stand as its well known large cities. Theamis shares its southern border with Aravae and its eastern border with Mulshear.

name: Tundra of Nalscour
description: The Tundra of Nalscour Tundra is the northernmost and harshest land in Myrdae, sealed off from Esgaar by the jagged Frowning Spine. Scattered across the wastes stand the Ice-shard Cairns — towering, crystalline burial sites of forgotten clans that shimmer beneath the moonlight said to absorb the light and call forth spectral spirit-beasts tied to the old clan bloodlines.

name: Uldrea
description: A northern territory once cut off from the rest of Myrdae by mysterious elemental forces Uldrea's southern border is shared with the Mahruud. Though its landscape varies, much of the region is marked by arid plains and the port city of Sulura now serves as a vital gateway into the reclaimed land.

name: Wheldrake
description: Wheldrake is the northeasternmost territory of the Bathaen Empire, a land of hills and marshes. Wheldrake is home to the legend of the Order of the Blade — a once-revered brotherhood of knights who upheld honor above imperial decree and vanished after defying an old corrupt king of the Bathaen Empire. Their final fortress is said to be hidden in the Wheldrake Hills, guarded by spectral sentinels and relics that await the return of a worthy bearer to restore their broken vow. Wheldrake and Halesworth share Basctdelm as a capital.

name: Yag'vur
description: Yag'vur is a small territory nestled against the southwestern slopes of the Vanapur Mountains. The territory shares borders with the Rostiena Foothills, the Dalasor Coast, and Aravae.

name: Yearning Vale
description: The Yearning Vale is an area northwest of the Mahruud, where sluggish rivers and swamplands stretch across from the southeast to feed into the Broken Sea with the great port city of Tratta. In the Yearning Slough, humanoids and monstrosities live in harmony under the watchful eye of the ancient grey dragon Zovraesdria.
